old_claim.note: Enterprise Resource Planning provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Early 1960s computerized material-planning software predates the distinct ERP category coined in 1990.

source_shape.source_locator: The history section states that Gartner coined ERP in 1990 for a new generation of MRP systems and traces their predecessors to 1960s inventory-control packages.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The ERP review separates 1960s inventory-control and MRP predecessors from the ERP category Gartner coined in 1990.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A category coined three decades after early planning software cannot be a prerequisite for that earlier software; ERP belongs downstream in the integration lineage.

ontology_before: Early supply-chain planning software incorrectly depended on its later ERP successor.
ontology_after: Early supply-chain planning software retains its 1960s operational-planning scope without a backward ERP dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: supply_chain_management_software no longer directly depends on enterprise_resource_planning.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped exclusively to post-1990 SCM suites built as ERP modules.
A primary source establishes a pre-1960 ERP category matching the current ERP scope.
short_reason: Early 1960s computerized material-planning software predates the distinct ERP category coined in 1990.
